H1.definition: a primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application.
H1.kjv: chief, (fore-) father(-less), [idiom] patrimony, principal. Compare names in 'Abi-'.

H3.definition: from the same as אָבִיב; a green plant.

H5.definition: of foreign origin; Abagtha, a eunuch of Xerxes.

H6.definition: a primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. lose oneself; by implication to perish (causative, destroy).
H6.kjv: break, destroy(-uction), [phrase] not escape, fail, lose, (cause to, make) perish, spend, [idiom] and surely, take, be undone, [idiom] utterly, be void of, have no way to flee.

H8.definition: active of participle of אָבַד; (concrete) wretched or (abstract) destruction.

H9.definition: from אָבַד; Compare אֲבַדֹּה.; concrete, something lost; abstract, destruction, i.e. Hades.

H10.definition: the same as אֲבֵדָה, miswritten for אֲבַדּוֹן; a perishing.

H11.definition: intensive from אָבַד; abstract, a perishing; concrete, Hades.

H14.definition: a primitive root; to breathe after, i.e. (figuratively) to be acquiescent.
H14.kjv: consent, rest content will, be willing.

H16.definition: from אָבָה (in the sense of bending toward); the papyrus.

H17.definition: from אָבָה (in the sense of desiring); want.

H18.definition: from אָבַס; a manger or stall.

H19.definition: from an unused root (apparently meaning to turn); brandishing of a sword.

H20.definition: of uncertain derivation; a melon (only plural).

H21.definition: from אָב; fatherly; Abi, Hezekiah's mother.

H22.definition: from אָב and אֵל; father (i.e. possessor) of God; Abiel, the name of two Israelites.

H23.definition: from אָב and אָסַף; father of gathering (i.e. gatherer); Abiasaph, an Israelite.

H24.definition: from an unused root (meaning to be tender); green, i.e. a young ear of grain; hence, the name of the month Abib or Nisan.
H24.kjv: Abib, ear, green ears of corn (not maize).

H25.definition: from אָב and גִּבְעוֹן; father (i.e. founder) of Gibon; Abi-Gibon, perhaps an Israelite.

H26.definition: or shorter אֲבִיגַל; from אָב and גִּיל; father (i.e. source) of joy; Abigail or Abigal, the name of two Israelitesses.

H27.definition: from אָב and דִּין; father of judgment (i.e. judge); Abidan, an Israelite.

H28.definition: from אָב and יָדַע; father of knowledge (i.e. knowing); Abida, a son of Abraham by Keturah.

H29.definition: or prolonged אֲבִיָּהוּ ; from אָב and יָהּ; father (i.e. worshipper) of Jah; Abijah, the name of several Israelite men and two Israelitesses.

H30.definition: from אָב and הוּא; father (i.e. worshipper) of Him (i.e. God); Abihu, a son of Aaron.

H31.definition: from אָב and הוֹד; father (i.e. possessor) of renown; Abihud, the name of two Israelites.

H32.definition: or (more correctly) אֲבִיחַיִל ; from אָב and חַיִל; father (i.e. possessor) of might; Abihail or Abichail, the name of three Israelites and two Israelitesses.

H33.definition: from אֲבִיעֶזֶר with the article inserted; father of the Ezrite; an Abiezrite or descendant of Abiezer.

H34.definition: from אָבָה, in the sense of want (especially in feeling); destitute.

H35.definition: from אָבָה; provocative of desire; the caper berry (from its stimulative taste).

H36.definition: from אָב and טוּב; father of goodness (i.e. good); Abitub, an Israelite.

H37.definition: from אָב and טַל; father of dew (i.e. fresh); Abital, a wife of King David.

H38.definition: from אָב and יָם; father of (the) sea (i.e. seaman); Abijam (or Abijah), a king of Judah.

H39.definition: from אָב and an elsewhere unused (probably foreign) word; father of Mael (apparently some Arab tribe); Abimael, a son of Joktan.

H40.definition: from אָב and מֶלֶךְ; father of (the) king; Abimelek, the name of two Philistine kings and of two Israelites.

H41.definition: from אָב and נָדַב; father of generosity (i.e. liberal); Abinadab, the name of four Israelites.

H42.definition: from אָב and נֹעַם; father of pleasantness (i.e. gracious); Abinoam, an Israelite.

H43.definition: contracted from אֲבִיאָסָף; Ebjasaph, an Israelite.

H44.definition: from אָב and עֵזֶר; father of help (i.e. helpful); Abiezer, the name of two Israelites.

H45.definition: from אָב and an unused root of uncertain derivation; probably, father of strength (i.e. valiant); Abialbon, an Israelite.

H48.definition: from אָב and רוּם; father of height (i.e. lofty); Abiram, the name of two Israelites.

H49.definition: from אָב and שָׁגָה; father of error (i.e. blundering); Abishag, a concubine of David.

H50.definition: xlit ʼĂbîyshûwac corrected to ʼĂbîyshûwaʻ; from אָב and שׁוֹעַ; father of plenty (i.e. prosperous); Abishua, the name of two Israelites.

H51.definition: from אָב and שׁוּר; father of (the) wall (i.e. perhaps mason); Abishur, an Israelite.

H52.definition: or (shorter) אַבְשַׁי ; from אָב and שַׁי; father of a gift (i.e. probably generous); Abishai, an Israelite.

H53.definition: or (shortened) אַבְשָׁלוֹם ; from אָב and שָׁלוֹם; father of peace (i.e. friendly); Abshalom, a son of David; also (the fuller form) a later Israelite.

H54.definition: contracted from אָב and יָתַר; father of abundance (i.e. liberal); Ebjathar, an Israelite.

H55.definition: a primitive root; probably to coil upward.

H58.definition: from an unused root (meaning to be grassy); a meadow.
H58.kjv: plain. Compare also the proper names beginning with Abel-.

H59.definition: from אָבֵל; a meadow; Abel, the name of two places in Palestine..

H61.definition: apparently from אָבַל through the idea of negation; nay, i.e. truly or yet.

H62.definition: from אָבֵל and בַּיִת and מַעֲכָה; meadow of Beth-Maakah; Abel of Beth-maakah, a place in Palestine.

H63.definition: from אָבֵל and the plural of שִׁטָּה, with the article inserted; meadow of the acacias; Abel hash-Shittim, a place in Palestine.

H64.definition: from אָבֵל and the plural of כֶּרֶם; meadow of vineyards; Abel-Keramim, a place in Palestine.

H65.definition: from אָבֵל and מְחֹלָהxlit mᵉchôwlâh corrected to mᵉchôlâh; meadow of dancing; Abel-Mecholah, a place in Palestine.

H66.definition: from אָבֵל and מַיִם; meadow of water; Abel-Majim, a place in Palestine.

H67.definition: from אָבֵל and מִצְרַיִם; meadow of Egypt; Abel-Mitsrajim, a place in Palestine.

H68.definition: from the root of בָּנָה through the meaning to build; a stone.
H68.kjv: [phrase] carbuncle, [phrase] mason, [phrase] plummet, (chalk-, hail-, head-, sling-) stone(-ny), (divers) weight(-s).

H70.definition: from the same as אֶבֶן; a pair of stones (only dual); a potter's wheel or a midwife's stool (consisting alike of two horizontal disks with a support between).

H71.definition: perhaps feminine of אֶבֶן; stony;  Compare אֲמָנָה.; Abanah, a river near Damascus.

H72.definition: from אֶבֶן and עֵזֶר with the article inserted; stone of the help; Eben-ha-Ezer, a place in Palestine.

H74.definition: or (fully) אֲבִינֵר ; from אָב and נִיר; father of light (i.e. enlightening); Abner, an Israelite.

H76.definition: (by reduplication) from an unused root (meaning to belch forth); an inflammatory pustule (as eruption).

H77.definition: from an unused root probably meaning to gleam; conspicuous; Ebets, a place in Palestine.

H78.definition: from the same as אֲבַעְבֻּעָה; splendid; Ibtsan, an Israelite.

H79.definition: a primitive root, probably to float away (as vapor), but used only as denominative from אָבָק; to bedust, i.e. grapple.

H80.definition: from root of אָבַק; light particles (as volatile).

H85.definition: contracted from אָב and an unused root (probably meaning to be populous); father of a multitude; Abraham, the later name of Abram.

H86.definition: probably an Egyptian word meaning; kneel.

H87.definition: contracted from אֲבִירָם; high father; Abram, the original name of Abraham.

H88.definition: plural of אוֹב; water-skins; Oboth, a place in the Desert.

H89.definition: of uncertain derivation (compare אֲגַג); Age, an Israelite.

H90.definition: or אֲגָג; of uncertain derivation (compare אָגֵא); flame; Agag, a title of Amalekitish kings.

H91.definition: patrial or patronymic from אֲגַג; an Agagite or descendent (subject) of Agag.

H92.definition: feminine passive participle of an unused root (meaning to bind); a band, bundle, knot, or arch.

H93.definition: prob of Persian origin; a nut.

H94.definition: passive participle of אָגַר; gathered (i.e. received among the sages); Agur, a fanciful name for Solomon.

H95.definition: from the same as אָגוּר; properly, something gathered, i.e. perhaps a grain or berry; used only of a small (silver) coin.

H96.definition: from an unused root (meaning to flow down or together as drops); a reservoir.

H97.definition: dual of אֶגֶל.; a double pond; Eglajim, a place in Moab.

H98.definition: from an unused root (meaning to collect as water); a marsh; hence a rush (as growing in swamps); hence a stockade of reeds.

H99.definition: probably from the same as אֲגַם (in the sense of stagnant water); figuratively, sad.

H100.definition: from the same as אֲגַם; a marshy pool (others from a different root, a kettle); by implication; a bulrush (as growing there); collectively a rope of bulrushes.

H101.definition: probably from נָגַן; a bowl (as pounded out hollow).

H102.definition: probably from נָגַף (through the idea of impending); a cover or heap; i.e.; (only plural) wings of an army, or crowds of troops.

H104.definition: (Aramaic) of Persian origin; an epistle (as carried by a state courier or postman).

H106.definition: from גָּרַף (in the sense of grasping); the clenched hand.

H108.definition: from the same as אוּד (in the sense of enveloping); a fog.

H110.definition: probably from אָדַב (in the sense of chastisement) and אֵל; disciplined of God; Adbeel, a son of Ishmael.

H111.definition: probably an orthographical variation for חֲדַד; Adad (or Hadad), an Edomite.

H112.definition: of uncertain derivation; Iddo, an Israelite.

H113.definition: or (shortened) אָדֹן; from an unused root (meaning to rule); sovereign, i.e. controller (human or divine).
H113.kjv: lord, master, owner. Compare also names beginning with 'Adoni-'.

H114.definition: probably intensive for אָדוֹן; powerful; Addon, apparently an Israelite.

H115.definition: dual from אָדַר (in the sense of eminence); double mound; Adorajim, a place in Palestine.

H117.definition: from אָדַר; wide or (generally) large; figuratively, powerful.
H117.kjv: excellent, famous, gallant, glorious, goodly, lordly, mighty(-ier one), noble, principal, worthy.

H118.definition: of Persian derivation; Adalja, a son of Haman.

H119.definition: to show blood (in the face), i.e.; flush or turn rosy.

H120.definition: from אָדַם; ruddy i.e. a human being (an individual or the species, mankind, etc.).
H120.kjv: [idiom] another, [phrase] hypocrite, [phrase] common sort, [idiom] low, man (mean, of low degree), person.

H121.definition: the same as אָדָם; Adam the name of the first man, also of a place in Palestine.

H123.definition: or (fully) אֱדוֹם ; from אָדֹם; red (see Genesis 25:25); Edom, the elder twin-brother of Jacob; hence the region (Idumaea) occupied by him.

H124.definition: from אָדַם; redness, i.e. the ruby, garnet, or some other red gem.

H126.definition: contracted for אֲדָמָה; earthy; Admah, a place near the Dead Sea.

H127.definition: from אָדַם; soil (from its general redness).

H128.definition: the same as אֲדָמָה; Adamah, a place in Palestine.

H129.definition: from אֲדָמָה; earthy; Adami, a place in Palestine.

H130.definition: or (fully) אֱדוֹמִי ; patronymic from אֱדֹם; See אֲרוֹמִי.; an Edomite, or descendants from (or inhabitants of) Edom.

H131.definition: plural of אָדָם; red spots; Adummim, a pass in Palestine.

H132.definition: or (fully) אַדְמוֹנִי ; from אָדַם; reddish (of the hair or the complexion).

H133.definition: probably of Persian derivation; Admatha, a Persian nobleman.

H134.definition: from the same as אָדוֹן (in the sense of strength); a basis (of a building, a column, etc.).

H135.definition: intensive from the same as אֶדֶן; firm; Addan, an Israelite.

H136.definition: an emphatic form of אָדוֹן; the Lord (used as a proper name of God only).

H137.definition: from אָדוֹן and בֶּזֶק; lord of Bezek; Adoni-Bezek; a Canaanitish king.

H138.definition: original (prolonged) אֲדֹנִיָּהוּ ; from אָדוֹן and יָהּ; lord (i.e. worshipper) of Jah; Adonijah, the name of three Israelites.

H139.definition: from אָדוֹן and צֶדֶק; lord of justice; Adoni-Tsedek, a Canaanitish king.

H140.definition: from אָדוֹן and קוּם; lord of rising (i.e. high); Adonikam, the name of one or two Israelites.

H141.definition: from אָדוֹן and רוּם; lord of height; Adoniram, an Israelite.

H142.definition: a primitive root; to expand, i.e. be great or (figuratively) magnificent.

H143.definition: probably of foreign derivation; perhaps meaning fire; Adar, the 12th Hebrew month.

H145.definition: from אָדַר; amplitude, i.e. (concrete) a mantle; also (figuratively) splendor.

H146.definition: intensive from אָדַר; ample; Addar, a place in Palestine; also an Israelite.

H147.definition: (Aramaic) intensive, from a root corresponding to אָדַר; ample, i.e. a threshing-floor.

H148.definition: (Aramaic) from the same as אִדַּר, and גְּזַר; a chief diviner, or astrologer.

H149.definition: (Aramaic) probably of Persian origin; quickly or carefully.

H150.definition: of Persian origin; a daric or Persian coin.

H151.definition: contracted for אֲדֹנִירָם; Adoram (or Adoniram), an Israelite.

H152.definition: from אָדַר and מֶלֶךְ; splendor of (the) king; Adrammelek, the name of an Assyrian idol, also of a son of Sennacherib.

H153.definition: (Aramaic) an orthographical variation for דְּרַע; an arm, i.e. (figuratively) power.

H154.definition: from the equivalent of אֶדְרָע; mighty; Edrei, the name of two places in Palestine.

H155.definition: feminine of אַדִּיר; ; also the same as אֶדֶר; something ample (as a large vine, a wide dress).

H156.definition: a primitive root; to tread out (grain).

H157.definition: or אָהֵב ; a primitive root; to have affection for (sexually or otherwise).

H158.definition: from אָהַב; affection (in a good or a bad sense).

H161.definition: from an unused root meaning to be united; unity; Ohad, an Israelite.

H162.definition: apparently a primitive word expressing pain exclamatorily; Oh!.

H163.definition: probably of foreign origin; Ahava, a river of Babylonia.

H164.definition: from the same as אֹהַד; united; Ehud, the name of two or three Israelites.

H165.definition: apparently an orthographical variation for אַיֵּה; where.
H165.kjv: I will be (Hosea 13:10,14) (which is often the rendering of the same Hebrew form from הָיָה).

H166.definition: a primitive root; to be clear.

H167.definition: a denominative from אֹהֶל; to tent.

H168.definition: from אָהַל; a tent (as clearly conspicuous from a distance).

H169.definition: the same as אֹהֶל; Ohel, an Israelite.

H170.definition: in form a feminine of אֹהֶל, but in fact for אׇהֳלָהּ; from אֹהֶל; her tent (i.e. idolatrous sanctuary); Oholah, a symbolic name for Samaria.

H171.definition: from אֹהֶל and אָב; tent of (his) father; Oholiab, an Israelite.

H172.definition: (similarly with אׇהֳלָה) for אׇהֳלִיבָהּ ; from אֹהֶל; my tent (is) in her; Oholibah, a symbolic name for Judah.

H173.definition: from אֹהֶל and בָּמָה; tent of (the) height; Oholibamah, a wife of Esau.

H174.definition: or (feminine) אֲהָלוֹת (only used thus in the plural); of foreign origin; aloe wood (i.e. sticks).

H175.definition: of uncertain derivation; Aharon, the brother of Moses.

H176.definition: presumed to be the 'constructive' or genitival form of אַו ; short for אַוָּה; desire (and so probably in Proverbs 31:4); hence (by way of alternative) or, also if.
H176.kjv: also, and, either, if, at the least, [idiom] nor, or, otherwise, then, whether.

H177.definition: from אוֹ and אֵל; wish of God; Uel, and Israelite.

H178.definition: from the same as אָב (apparently through the idea of prattling a father's name); properly, a mumble, i.e. a water skin (from its hollow sound); hence a necromancer (ventriloquist, as from a jar).

H179.definition: probably from אָבַל; mournful; Obil, an Ishmaelite.

H180.definition: or (shortened) אֻבָל ; from יָבַל (in the sense of יָבָל); a stream.

H181.definition: from an unused root meaning to rake together; a poker (for turning or gathering embers).

H182.definition: or (shortened) אֹדוֹת (only thus in the plural); from the same as אוּד; turnings (i.e. occasions); (adverb) on account of.

H183.definition: a primitive root; to wish for.
H183.kjv: covet, (greatly) desire, be desirous, long, lust (after).

H184.definition: a primitive root; to extend or mark out.

H186.definition: perhaps by permutation for עֻזִּי, strong; Uzai, an Israelite.

H187.definition: of uncertain derivation; Uzal, a son of Joktan.

H188.definition: probably from אָוָה (in the sense of crying out after); lamentation; also interjectionally Oh!.

H189.definition: probably from אָוָה; desirous; Evi, a Midianitish chief.

H191.definition: from an unused root (meaning to be perverse); (figuratively) silly.

H192.definition: of Aramaic derivation and probably meaning soldier of Merodak; Evil-Merodak, a Babylonian king.

H193.definition: from an unused root meaning to twist, i.e. (by implication) be strong; the body (as being rolled together); also powerful.

H194.definition: or (shortened) אֻלַי; from אוֹ; if not; hence perhaps.
H194.kjv: if so be, may be, peradventure, unless.

H195.definition: of Persian derivation; the Ulai (or Eulaeus), a river of Persia.

H196.definition: from אֱוִיל; silly, foolish; hence (morally) impious.

H197.definition: or (shortened), אֻלָם ; from אָלַם (in the sense of tying); a vestibule (as bound to the building).

H198.definition: appar, from אָלַם (in the sense of dumbness); solitary; Ulam, the name of two Israelites.

H199.definition: apparently a variation of אוּלַי; however or on the contrary.
H199.kjv: as for, but, howbeit, in very deed, surely, truly, wherefore.

H200.definition: from the same as אֱוִיל; silliness.

H201.definition: from אָמַר; talkative; Omar, a grandson of Esau.

H202.definition: probably from the same as אָוֶן (in the sense of effort, but successful); ability, power, (figuratively) wealth.

H203.definition: the same as אוֹן; On, an Israelite.

H204.definition: or (shortened); אֹן; of Egyptian derivation; On, a city of Egypt.

H205.definition: from an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant (hence, to exert oneself, usually in vain; to come to naught); strictly nothingness; also trouble. vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol.
H205.kjv: affliction, evil, false, idol, iniquity, mischief, mourners(-ing), naught, sorrow, unjust, unrighteous, vain, vanity, wicked(-ness). Compare אַיִן.

H206.definition: the same as אָוֶן; idolatry; Aven, the contemptuous synonym of three places, one in Coele-Syria, one in Egypt (On), and one in Palestine (Bethel).
H206.kjv: Aven. See also אוֹן, בֵּית אָוֶן.

H207.definition: or (shortened) אֹנוֹ; prolonged from אוֹן; strong; Ono, a place in Palestine.

H208.definition: a variation of אוֹנָן; strong; Onam, the name of an Edomite and of an Israelite.

H209.definition: a variation of אוֹנוֹ; strong; Onan, a son of Judah.

H210.definition: perhaps a corruption of אוֹפִיר; Uphaz, a famous gold region.

H211.definition: or (shortened) אֹפִיר; and אוֹפִר; of uncertain derivation; Ophir, the name of a son of Joktan, and of a gold region in the East.

H212.definition: or (shortened) אֹפָן; from an unused root meaning to revolve; a wheel.

H213.definition: a primitive root; to press; (by implication) to be close, hurry, withdraw.

H215.definition: a primitive root; to be (causative, make) luminous (literally and metaphorically).
H215.kjv: [idiom] break of day, glorious, kindle, (be, en-, give, show) light (-en, -ened), set on fire, shine.

H216.definition: from אוֹר; illumination or (concrete) luminary (in every sense, including lightning, happiness, etc.).
H216.kjv: bright, clear, [phrase] day, light (-ning), morning, sun.

H217.definition: from אוֹר; flame; hence (in the plural) the East (as being the region of light).

H218.definition: the same as אוּר; Ur, a place in Chaldaea; also an Israelite.

H219.definition: feminine of אוֹר; luminousness, i.e. (figuratively) prosperity; also a plant (as being bright).

H220.definition: by transposition for אֻרְוָה; a stall.

H221.definition: from אוּר; fiery; Uri, the name of three Israelites.

H222.definition: from אוּר and אֵל; flame of God; Uriel, the name of two Israelites.

H223.definition: or (prolonged) אוּרִיָּהוּ ; from אוּר and יָהּ; flame of Jah; Urijah, the name of one Hittite and five Israelites.

H224.definition: plural of אוּר; lights; Urim, the oracular brilliancy of the figures in the high-priest's breastplate.

H225.definition: a primitive root; properly, to come, i.e. (implied) to assent.

H226.definition: probably from אוּת (in the sense of appearing); a signal (literally or figuratively), as aflag, beacon, monument, omen, prodigy, evidence, etc..

H227.definition: a demonstrative adverb; at that time or place; also as a conjunction, therefore.
H227.kjv: beginning, for, from, hitherto, now, of old, once, since, then, at which time, yet.

H228.definition: (Aramaic) or אֲזָה (Aramaic); to kindle; (by implication) to heat.

H229.definition: probably from אֵזוֹב; hyssop-like; Ezbai, an Israelite.

H232.definition: from אֲזִקִּים; something girt; a belt, also a band.

H233.definition: probably from אָז; at that time.

H234.definition: from זָכַר; a reminder; specifically remembrance-offering.

H235.definition: a primitive root; to go away, hence, to disappear.
H235.kjv: fail, gad about, go to and fro (but in Ezekiel 27:19 the word is rendered by many 'from Uzal,' by others 'yarn'), be gone (spent).

H236.definition: (Aramaic) the same as אָזַל; to depart.

H237.definition: from אָזַל; departure; Ezel, a memorial stone in Palestine.

H238.definition: a primitive root; probably to expand; but used only as a denominative from אֹזֶן; to broaden out the ear (with the hand), i.e. (by implication) to listen.
H238.kjv: give (perceive by the) ear, hear(-ken). See אָזַן.

H239.definition: a primitive root (rather identical with אָזַן through the idea of scales as if two ears); to weigh, i.e. (figuratively) ponder.

H240.definition: from אָזַן; a spade or paddle (as having a broad end).

H241.definition: from אָזַן; broadness. i.e. (concrete) the ear (from its form in man).
H241.kjv: [phrase] advertise, audience, [phrase] displease, ear, hearing, [phrase] show.

H242.definition: from אָזַן and שֶׁאֱרָה; plot of Sheerah (i.e. settled by him); Uzzen-Sheerah, a place in Palestine.

H243.definition: from אָזַן and תָּבוֹר; flats (i.e. tops) of Tabor (i.e. situated on it); Aznoth-Tabor, a place in Palestine.

H244.definition: from אֹזֶן; having (quick) ears; Ozni, an Israelite; also an Oznite (collectively), his descendant.

H245.definition: from אָזַן and יָהּ; heard by Jah; Azanjah, an Israelite.

H248.definition: a variation for זְרוֹעַ; the arm.

H249.definition: from זָרַח (in the sense of springing up); a spontaneous growth, i.e. native (tree or persons).
H249.kjv: bay tree, (home-) born (in the land), of the (one's own) country (nation).

H250.definition: patronymic from חֹבָב; an Ezrachite or descendant of Zerach.

H251.definition: a primitive word; a brother (used in the widest sense of literal relationship and metaphorical affinity or resemblance (like father)); another, brother(-ly).
H251.kjv: kindred, like, other. Compare also the proper names beginning with 'Ah-' or 'Ahi-'.

H253.definition: a variation for אֲהָהּ; Oh! (expressive of grief or surprise).

H254.definition: of uncertain derivation; a fire-pot or chafing dish.

H255.definition: probably from אָח; a howler or lonesome wild animal.

H256.definition: once (by contraction) אֶחָב (Jeremiah 29:22); from אָח and אָב; brother (i.e. friend) of (his) father; Achab, the name of a king of Israel and of a prophet at Babylon.

H257.definition: from אָח and בִּין; brother (i.e. possessor) of understanding; Achban, an Israelite.

H258.definition: perhaps a primitive root; to unify, i.e. (figuratively) collect (one's thoughts).

H259.definition: a numeral from אָחַד; properly, united, i.e. one; or (as an ordinal) first.
H259.kjv: a, alike, alone, altogether, and, any(-thing), apiece, a certain, (dai-) ly, each (one), [phrase] eleven, every, few, first, [phrase] highway, a man, once, one, only, other, some, together,

H260.definition: of uncertain (perhaps Egyptian) derivation; a bulrush or any marshy grass (particularly that along the Nile).

H261.definition: from אָחַד; united; Echud, the name of three Israelites.

H262.definition: from חָוָה (in the sense of חֲוָא); an utterance.

H265.definition: by reduplication from אָח; brotherly; Achoach, an Israelite.

H266.definition: patronymic from אַחֲוָה; an Achochite or descendant of Achoach.

H267.definition: perhaps from אָח and מַיִם; brother (i.e. neighbour) of water; Achumai, an Israelite.

H268.definition: or (shortened) אָחֹר ; from אֲחִירַע; the hinder part; hence (adverb) behind, backward; also (as facing north) the West.
H268.kjv: after(-ward), back (part, -side, -ward), hereafter, (be-) hind(-er part), time to come, without.

H269.definition: irregular feminine of אָח; a sister (used very widely (like brother), literally and figuratively).

H270.definition: a primitive root; to seize (often with the accessory idea of holding in possession).
H270.kjv: [phrase] be affrighted, bar, (catch, lay, take) hold (back), come upon, fasten, handle, portion, (get, have or take) possess(-ion).

H271.definition: from אָחַז; possessor; Achaz, the name of a Jewish king and of an Israelite.

H272.definition: feminine passive participle from אָחַז; something seized, i.e. a possession (especially of land).

H273.definition: from אָחַז; seizer; Achzai, an Israelite.

H274.definition: or (prolonged) אֲחַזְיָהוּ ; from אָחַז and יָהּ; Jah has seized; Achazjah, the name of a Jewish and an Israelite king.

H275.definition: from אָחַז; seizure; Achuzzam, an Israelite.

H276.definition: a variation of אֲחֻזָּה; possession; Achuzzath, a Philistine.

H277.definition: from אָח; brotherly; Achi, the name of two Israelites.

H278.definition: probably the same as אֲחִי; Echi, an Israelite.

H279.definition: from אָח and אֵם; brother of the mother (i.e. uncle); Achiam, an Israelite.

H281.definition: or (prolonged) אֲחִיָּהוּ ; from אָח and יָהּ; brother (i.e. worshipper) of Jah; Achijah, the name of nine Israelites.

H282.definition: from אָח and הוֹד; brother (i.e. possessor) of renown; Achihud, an Israelite.

H283.definition: prolonged from אָח; brotherly; Achio, the name of three Israelites.

H284.definition: from אָח and חוּד; brother of a riddle (i.e. mysterious); Achichud, an Israelite.

H285.definition: from אָח and טוּב; brother of goodness; Achitub, the name of several priests.

H286.definition: from אָח and יָלַד; brother of one born; Achilud, an Israelite.

H287.definition: from אָח and מוּת; brother of death; Achimoth, an Israelite.

H288.definition: from אָח and מֶלֶךְ; brother of (the) king; Achimelek, the name of an Israelite and of a Hittite.

H289.definition: or אֲחִימָן ; from אָח and מִן; brother of a portion (i.e. gift); Achiman, the name of an Anakite and of an Israelite.

H290.definition: from אָח and the equivalent of מַעַץ; brother of anger; Achimaats, the name of three Israelites.

H291.definition: from אָח; brotherly; Achjan, an Israelite.

H292.definition: from אָח and נָדַב; brother of liberality; Achinadab, an Israelite.

H293.definition: from אָח and נֹעַם; brother of pleasantness; Achinoam, the name of two Israelitesses.

H294.definition: from אָח and סָמַךְ; brother of support; Achisamak, an Israelite.

H295.definition: from אָח and עֵזֶר; brother of help; Achiezer, the name of two Israelites.

H296.definition: from אָח and קוּם; brother of rising (i.e. high); Achikam, an Israelite.

H297.definition: from אָח and רוּם; brother of height (i.e. high); Achiram, an Israelite.

H298.definition: patronymic from אֲחִירָם; an Achiramite or descendant (collectively) of Achiram.

H299.definition: from אָח and רַע; brother of wrong; Achira, an Israelite.

H300.definition: from אָח and שַׁחַר; brother of (the) dawn; Achishachar, an Israelite.

H301.definition: from אָח and שִׁיר; brother of (the) singer; Achishar, an Israelite.

H302.definition: from אָח and תָּפֵל; brother of folly; Achithophel, an Israelite.

H303.definition: from the same root as חֶלֶב; fatness (i.e. fertile); Achlab, a place in Palestine.

H304.definition: the same as אַחֲלַי; wishful; Achlai, the name of an Israelitess and of an Israelite.

H305.definition: or אַחֲלֵי; prob from אָח and a variation of לוּא; would that!.

H306.definition: perhaps from חָלַם (and thus dream-stone); a gem, probably the amethyst.

H307.definition: of Persian derivation; Achmetha (i.e. Ecbatana), the summer capital of Persia.

H308.definition: of uncertain derivation; Achasbai, an Israelite.

H309.definition: a primitive root; to loiter (i.e. be behind); by implication to procrastinate.
H309.kjv: continue, defer, delay, hinder, be late (slack), stay (there), tarry (longer).

H310.definition: from אָחַר; properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after (in various senses).
H310.kjv: after (that, -ward), again, at, away from, back (from, -side), behind, beside, by, follow (after, -ing), forasmuch, from, hereafter, hinder end, [phrase] out (over) live, [phrase] persecute, posterity, pursuing, remnant, seeing, since, thence(-forth), when, with.

H312.definition: from אָחַר; properly, hinder; generally, next, other, etc..

H313.definition: the same as אַחֵר; Acher, an Israelite.

H314.definition: or (shortened) אַחֲרֹן; from אָחַר; hinder; generally, late or last; specifically (as facing the east) western.
H314.kjv: after (-ward), to come, following, hind(-er, -ermost, -most), last, latter, rereward, ut(ter) most.

H315.definition: from אַחַר and אָח; after (his) brother; Achrach, an Israelite.

H316.definition: from אַחַר and חֵיל; behind (the) intrenchment (i.e. safe); Acharchel, an Israelite.

H319.definition: from אַחַר; the last or end, hence, the future; also posterity.
H319.kjv: (last, latter) end (time), hinder (utter) -most, length, posterity, remnant, residue, reward.

H320.definition: (Aramaic) from אַחַר; the same as אַחֲרִית; later.

H321.definition: (Aramaic) from אַחַר; the same as אׇחֳרִי; other.

H323.definition: of Persian derivation; a satrap or governorof amain province (of Persia).

H325.definition: or (shortened) אַחַשְׁרֹשׁ (Esther 10:1); of Persian origin; Achashverosh (i.e. Ahasuerus or Artaxerxes, but in this case Xerxes), the title (rather than name) of a Persian king.

H326.definition: probably of Persian derivation; an achastarite (i.e. courier); the designation (rather than name) of an Israelite.

H328.definition: from an unused root perhaps meaning to move softly; (as a noun) a necromancer (from their soft incantations), (as an adverb) gently.

H329.definition: from an unused root probably meaning to pierce or make fast; a thorn-tree (especially the buckthorn).

H330.definition: from an unused root (probably meaning to bind); properly, twisted (yarn), i.e. tapestry.

H331.definition: a primitive root; to close (the lips or ears); by analology to contract (a window by bevelled jambs).

H332.definition: a primitive root; to close up.

H333.definition: from אָטַר; maimed; Ater, the name of three Israelites.

H334.definition: from אָטַר; shut up, i.e. impeded (as to the use of the right hand).

H335.definition: perhaps from אַיִן; where? hence how?.
H335.kjv: how, what, whence, where, whether, which (way).

H336.definition: lemma אי missing vowel, corrected to אִי; probably identical with אַי (through the idea of a query); not.

H338.definition: probably identical with אִי (through the idea of a doleful sound); a howler (used only in the plural), i.e. any solitary wild creature.

H339.definition: from אָוָה; properly, a habitable spot (as desirable); dry land, a coast, an island.

H340.definition: a primitive root; to hate (as one of an opposite tribe or party); hence to be hostile.

H341.definition: or (fully) אוֹיֵב; active participle of אָיַב; hating; an adversary.

H343.definition: from the same as אוּד (in the sense of bending down); oppression; by implication misfortune, ruin.

H344.definition: perhaps from אִי; the screamer, i.e. a hawk.

H345.definition: the same as אַיָּה; Ajah, the name of two Israelites.

H347.definition: from אָיַב; hated (i.e. persecuted); Ijob, the patriarch famous for his patience.

H348.definition: from אִיlemma אי missing vowel, corrected to אִי and זְבֻל; Izebel, the wife of king Ahab.

H349.definition: also אֵיכָה ; and אֵיכָכָה ; prolonged from אַי; how? or how!; also where.

H350.definition: from אִיlemma אי missing vowel, corrected to אִי and כָּבוֹד; (there is) no glory, i.e. inglorious; Ikabod, a son of Phineas.

H351.definition: probably a variation for אֵיךְ, but not as an interogative; where.

H352.definition: from the same as אוּל; properly, strength; hence, anything strong; specifically a chief (politically); also a ram (from his strength); a pilaster (as a strong support); an oak or other strong tree.
H352.kjv: mighty (man), lintel, oak, post, ram, tree.

H354.definition: an intensive form of אַיִל (in the sense of ram); a stag or male deer.

H355.definition: feminine of אַיָּל; a doe or female deer.

H356.definition: or (shortened) אֵלוֹן ; or אֵילֹן ; from אַיִל; oak-grove; Elon, the name of a place in Palestine, and also of one Hittite, two Israelites.

H357.definition: from אַיָּל; deer-field; Ajalon, the name of five places in Palestine.

H358.definition: from אֵילוֹן, בַּיִת, and חָנַן; oak-grove of (the) house of favor; Elon of Beth-chanan, a place in Palestine.

H359.definition: or אֵילַת ; from אַיִל; trees or a grove (i.e. palms); Eloth or Elath, a place on the Red Sea.

H360.definition: feminine of אֱיָל; power; by implication, protection.

H361.definition: or (shortened) אֵלָם ; or (feminine) אֵלַמָּה ; probably from אַיִל; a pillar-space (or colonnade), i.e. a pale (or portico).

H362.definition: plural of אַיִל; palm-trees; Elim, a place in the Desert.

H364.definition: from אַיִל and פָּארָן; oak of Paran; El-Paran, a portion of the district of Paran.

H365.definition: the same as אַיָּלָה; a doe.

H366.definition: from an unused root (meaning to frighten); frightful.

H367.definition: or (shortened) אֵמָה; from the same as אָיֹם; fright; concrete, an idol (as a bugbear).
H367.kjv: dread, fear, horror, idol, terrible, terror.

H368.definition: plural of אֵימָה; terrors; Emim, an early Canaanitish (or Maobitish) tribe.

H369.definition: as if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
H369.kjv: else, except, fail, (father-) less, be gone, in(-curable), neither, never, no (where), none, nor, (any, thing), not, nothing, to nought, past, un(-searchable), well-nigh, without. Compare אַיִן.

H370.definition: probably identical with אַיִן in the sense of query (compare אִיlemma אי missing vowel, corrected to אִי); where? (only in connection with prepositional prefix, whence).

H371.definition: apparently a shortened form of אַיִן; but (like אַיִן) an interrogative; is it not?.

H372.definition: from אִיlemma אי missing vowel, corrected to אִי and עֵזֶר; helpless; Iezer, an Israelite.

H373.definition: patronymic from אִיעֶזֵר; an Iezrite or descendant of Iezer.

H374.definition: or (shortened) אֵפָה ; of Egyptian derivation; an ephah or measure for grain; hence, a measure in general.

H375.definition: from אַי and פֹּה; what place?; also (of time) when?; or (of means) how?.

H376.definition: contracted for אֱנוֹשׁ (or perhaps rather from an unused root meaning to be extant); a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term (and in such cases frequently not expressed in translation).
H376.kjv: also, another, any (man), a certain, [phrase] champion, consent, each, every (one), fellow, (foot-, husband-) man, (good-, great, mighty) man, he, high (degree), him (that is), husband, man(-kind), [phrase] none, one, people, person, [phrase] steward, what (man) soever, whoso(-ever), worthy. Compare אִשָּׁה.

H377.definition: denominative from אִישׁ; to be a man, i.e. act in amanly way.

H378.definition: from אִישׁ and בֹּשֶׁת; man of shame; Ish-Bosheth, a son of King Saul.

H379.definition: from אִישׁ and הוֹד; man of renown; Ishod, an Israelite.

H380.definition: diminutive from אִישׁ; the little man of the eye; the pupil or ball; hence, the middle (of night).

H381.definition: from אִישׁ and חַיִל; man of might; by defective transcription (2 Samuel 23:20) אִישׁ־חַיxlit ʼÎsh-Chay corrected to ʼÎysh-Chay; as if from אִישׁ and חַי; living man; Ishchail (or Ish-chai), an Israelite.

H382.definition: from אִישׁ and טוֹב; man of Tob; Ish-Tob, a place in Palestine.

H383.definition: (Aramaic) corresponding to יֵשׁ; properly, entity; used only as aparticle of affirmation, there is.
H383.kjv: art thou, can, do ye, have, it be, there is (are), [idiom] we will not.

H384.definition: perhaps from אֹשֶׁר and אֵל; God has arrived; Ithiel, the name of an Israelite, also of a symbolical person.

H385.definition: from אִי and תָּמָר; coast of the palm-tree; Ithamar, a son of Aaron.

H386.definition: or (shortened) אֵתָן ; from an unused root (meaning to continue); permanence; hence (concrete) permanent; specifically a chieftain.

H387.definition: the same as אֵיתָן; permanent; Ethan, the name of four Israelites.

H388.definition: plural of אֵיתָן; always with the article; the permanent brooks; Ethanim, the name of a month.

H389.definition: akin to אָכֵן; a particle of affirmation, surely; hence (by limitation) only.
H389.kjv: also, in any wise, at least, but, certainly, even, howbeit, nevertheless, notwithstanding, only, save, surely, of a surety, truly, verily, [phrase] wherefore, yet (but).

H390.definition: from an unused root probably meaning to strengthen; a fortress; Accad, a place in Babylon.

H391.definition: from כָּזַב; falsehood; by implication treachery.

H392.definition: from אַכְזָב; deceitful (in the sense of a winter-torrent which fails in summer); Akzib, the name of two places in Palestine.

H393.definition: from an unused root (apparently meaning to act harshly); violent; by implication deadly; also (in a good sense) brave.

H396.definition: feminine from אָכַל; something eatable, i.e. food.

H397.definition: of uncertain derivation; Akish, a Philistine king.

H398.definition: a primitive root; to eat (literally or figuratively).
H398.kjv: [idiom] at all, burn up, consume, devour(-er, up), dine, eat(-er, up), feed (with), food, [idiom] freely, [idiom] in...wise(-deed, plenty), (lay) meat, [idiom] quite.

H400.kjv: eating, food, meal(-time), meat, prey, victuals.

H401.definition: or אֻכָּל; apparently from אָכַל; devoured; Ucal, a fancy name.

H403.definition: from כּוּן (compare כֵּן); firmly; figuratively, surely; also (advers.) but.
H403.kjv: but, certainly, nevertheless, surely, truly, verily.

H404.definition: a primitive root; apparently meaning to curve (as with a burden); to urge.

H405.definition: from אָכַף; a load; by implication, a stroke (others dignity).

H406.definition: from an unused root meaning to dig; a farmer.

H407.definition: from כָּשַׁף; fascination; Acshaph, a place in Palestine.

H408.definition: a negative particle (akin to לֹא); not (the qualified negation, used as a deprecative); once (Job 24:25) as a noun, nothing.
H408.kjv: nay, neither, [phrase] never, no, nor, not, nothing (worth), rather than.

H410.definition: shortened from אַיִל; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty (but used also of any deity).
H410.kjv: God (god), [idiom] goodly, [idiom] great, idol, might(-y one), power, strong. Compare names in '-el.'

H411.definition: a demonstrative particle (but only in a plural sense); these or those.

H413.definition: (but only used in the shortened constructive form אֶל ); a primitive particle; properly, denoting motion towards, but occasionally used of a quiescent position, i.e.; near, with or among; often in general, to.
H413.kjv: about, according to, after, against, among, as for, at, because(-fore, -side), both...and, by, concerning, for, from, [idiom] hath, in(-to), near, (out) of, over, through, to(-ward), under, unto, upon, whether, with(-in).

H414.definition: a variation of אֵלָהlemma אִלָה first vowel, corrected to אֵלָה; oak; Ela, an Israelite.

H415.definition: from אֵל and אֱלֹהִים and יִשְׂרָאֵל; the mighty god if Jisrael; El-Elohi-Jisrael, the title given to a consecrated spot by Jacob.

H416.definition: from אֵל and בֵּית־אֵל; the God of Bethel; El-Bethel, the title given to a consecrated spot by Jacob.

H417.definition: from אֵל and גָּבִישׁ; hail (as if a great pearl).

H418.definition: by transposition for אַלְמֻגִּים; sticks of algum wood.

H419.definition: from אֵל and דּוֹד; God has loved; Eldad, an Israelite.

H420.definition: from אֵל and יָדַע; God of knowledge; Eldaah, a son of Midian.

H421.definition: a primitive root (rather identical with אָלָה through the idea of invocation); to bewail.

H422.definition: a primitive root; properly, to adjure, i.e. (usually in a bad sense) imprecate.

H424.definition: lemma אִלָה first vowel, corrected to אֵלָה; feminine of אַיִל; an oak or other strong tree.

H425.definition: the same as אֵלָהlemma אִלָה first vowel, corrected to אֵלָה; Elah, the name of an Edomite, of four Israelites, and also of a place in Palestine.

H428.definition: prolonged from אֵל; these or those; an-(the) other.
H428.kjv: one sort, so, some, such, them, these (same), they, this, those, thus, which, who(-m).

H430.definition: plural of אֱלוֹהַּ; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a superlative.
H430.kjv: angels, [idiom] exceeding, God (gods) (-dess, -ly), [idiom] (very) great, judges, [idiom] mighty.

H432.definition: probably from אַל; nay, i.e. (softened) if.

H433.definition: rarely (shortened) אֱלֹהַּ; probably prolonged (emphatic) from אֵל; a deity or the Deity.

H435.definition: probably of foreign derivation; Elul, the sixth Jewish month.

H436.definition: prolonged from אַיִל; an oak or other strong tree.

H438.definition: the same as אַלּוֹן; Allon, an Israelite, also a place in Palestine.

H439.definition: from אַלּוֹן and a variation of בְּכִית; oak of weeping; Allon-Bakuth, a monumental tree.

H440.definition: or rather (shortened) אֵלֹנִי; patron from אַלּוֹן; an Elonite or descendant (collectively) of Elon.

H441.definition: or (shortened) אַלֻּף; from אָלַף; familiar; a friend, also gentle; hence, a bullock (as being tame; applied, although masculine, to a cow); and so, a chieftain (as notable, like neat cattle).
H441.kjv: captain, duke, (chief) friend, governor, guide, ox.

H442.definition: of uncertain derivation; Alush, a place in the Desert.

H443.definition: from אֵל and זָבַד; God has bestowed; Elzabad, the name of two Israelites.

H444.definition: a primitive root; to muddle, i.e. (figuratively and intransitive) to turn (morally) corrupt.

H445.definition: from אֵל and חָנַן; God (is) gracious; Elchanan, an Israelite.

H446.definition: from אֵל and אָב; God of (his) father; Eliab, the name of six Israelites.

H447.definition: from אֵל repeated; God of (his) God; Eliel, the name of nine Israelites.

H448.definition: or (contraction) אֱלִיָּתָה; from אֵל and אוּת; God of (his) consent; Eliathah, an Israelite.

H449.definition: from the same as אֶלְדָּד; God of (his) love; Elidad, an Israelite.

H450.definition: from אֵל and יָדַע; God (is) knowing; Eljada, the name of two Israelites and of an Aramaean leader.

H451.definition: from אָלָה (in the original sense of strength); the stout part, i.e. the fat tail of the Oriental sheep.

H452.definition: or prolonged אֵלִיָּהוּ; from אֵל and יָהּ; God of Jehovah; Elijah, the name of the famous prophet and of two other Israelites.

H453.definition: or (fully) אֱלִיהוּא; from אֵל and הוּא; God of him; Elihu, the name of one of Job's friends, and of three Israelites.

H454.definition: or (shortened) אֶלְיוֹעֵינַי; from אֵל and יְהֹוָה and עַיִן; towards Jehovah (are) my eyes; Eljehoenai or Eljoenai, the name of seven Israelites.

H455.definition: from אֵל and חָבָא; God will hide; Eljachba, an Israelite.

H456.definition: from אֵל and חֹרֶף; God of autumn; Elichoreph, an Israelite.

H457.definition: apparently from אַל; good for nothing, by anal. vain or vanity; specifically an idol.
H457.kjv: idol, no value, thing of nought.

H458.definition: from אֵל and מֶלֶךְ; God of (the) king; Elimelek, an Israelite.

H459.definition: (Aramaic) or shorter אִלֵּן; prolonged from אֵל; these.

H460.definition: from אֵל and יָסַף; God (is) gatherer; Eljasaph, the name of two Israelites.

H461.definition: from אֵל and עֵזֶר; God of help; Eliezer, the name of a Damascene and of ten Israelites.

H462.definition: probably contracted for אֶלְיְהוֹעֵינַי; Elienai, an Israelite.

H463.definition: from אֵל and עַם; God of (the) people; Eliam, an Israelite.

H464.definition: from אֵל and פָּז; God of gold; Eliphaz, the name of one of Job's friends, and of a son of Esau.

H465.definition: from אֵל and פָּלַל; God of judgment; Eliphal, an Israelite.

H466.definition: from אֵל and פָּלָה; God of his distinction; Eliphelehu, an Israelite.

H467.definition: or (shortened) אֱלְפֶּלֶט; from אֵל and פַּלֵּט; God of deliverance; Eliphelet or Elpelet, the name of six Israelites.

H468.definition: xlit ʼĔlêytsûwr corrected to ʼĔlîytsûwr; from אֵל and צוּר; God of (the) rock; Elitsur, an Israelite.

H469.definition: or (shortened) אֶלְצָפָן; from אֵל and צָפַן; God of treasure; Elitsaphan or Eltsaphan, an Israelite.

H470.definition: from אֵל and קוֹא; God of rejection; Elika, an Israelite.

H471.definition: from אֵל and קוּם; God of raising; Eljakim, the name of four Israelites.

H472.definition: from אֵל and שֶׁבַע (in the sense of שָׁבַע); God of (the) oath; Elisheba, the wife of Aaron.

H473.definition: probably of foreign derivation; Elishah, a son of Javan.

H474.definition: from אֵל and שׁוּעַ; God of supplication (or of riches); Elishua, the son of King David.

H475.definition: from אֵל and שׁוּב; God will restore; Eljashib, the name of six Israelites.

H476.definition: from אֵל and שָׁמַע; God of hearing; Elishama, the name of seven Israelites.

H477.definition: contracted for אֱלִישׁוּעַ.; Elisha, the famous prophet.

H478.definition: from אֵל and שָׁפַט; God of judgment; Elishaphat, an Israelite.

H481.definition: a primitive root; to tie fast; hence (of the mouth) to be tongue-tied.
H481.kjv: bind, be dumb, put to silence.

H484.definition: probably of foreign derivation (used thus only in the plural); almug (i.e. probably sandle-wood) sticks.

H485.definition: or (masculine) אָלֻם; passive participle of אָלַם; something bound; a sheaf.

H486.definition: probably of foreign derivation; Almodad, a son of Joktan.

H487.definition: from אַלָּה and מֶלֶךְ; oak of (the) king; Allammelek, a place in Palestine.

H488.definition: prolonged from אָלַם in the sense of bereavement; discarded (as a divorced person).

H489.definition: from אָלַם as in אַלְמָן; bereavement.

H490.definition: feminine of אַלְמָן; a widow; also a desolate place.

H491.definition: feminine of אַלְמָן; concrete, a widow; abstract, widowhood.

H492.definition: from אַלְמֹן in the sense of concealment; some one (i.e. so and so, without giving the name of the person or place).

H493.definition: from אֵל and נָעֵם; God (is his) delight; Elnaam, an Israelite.

H494.definition: from אֵל and נָתַן; God (is the) giver; Elnathan, the name of four Israelites.

H495.definition: probably of foreign derivation; Ellasar, an early country of Asia.

H496.definition: from אֵל and עוּד; God has testified; Elad, an Israelite.

H497.definition: from אֵל and עָדָה; God has decked; Eladah, an Israelite.

H498.definition: from אֵל and עוּז (in the sense of עֹז); God (is) defensive; Eluzai, an Israelite.

H499.definition: from אֵל and עָזַר; God (is) helper; Elazar, the name of seven Israelites.

H500.definition: or (more properly) אֶלְעָלֵה; from אֵל and עָלָה; God (is) going up; Elale or Elaleh, a place east of the Jordan.

H501.definition: from אֵל and עָשָׂה; God has made; Elasah, the name of four Israelites.

H502.definition: a primitive root, to associate with; hence, to learn (and causatively to teach).

H503.definition: denominative from אֶלֶף; causative, to make athousandfold.

H504.definition: from אָלַף; a family; also (from the sense of yoking or taming) an ox or cow.

H505.definition: prop, the same as אֶלֶף; hence (the ox's head being the first letter of the alphabet, and this eventually used as a numeral) a thousand.

H507.definition: the same as אֶלֶף; Eleph, a place in Palestine.

H508.definition: from אֵל and פָּעַל; God (is) act; Elpaal, an Israelite.

H510.definition: probably from אַל and קוּם; a non-rising (i.e. resistlessness).

H511.definition: from אֵל and קָנָה; God has obtained; Elkanah, the name of several Israelites.

H512.definition: patrial from a name of uncertain derivation; an Elkoshite or native of Elkosh.

H513.definition: probably from אֵל and a masculine form of תּוֹלְדָה (compare תּוֹלָד); God (is) generator; Eltolad, a place in Palestine.

H514.definition: xlit ʼEltᵉqê corrected to ʼEltᵉqêʼ; or (more properly) אֶלְתְּקֵה; of uncertain derivation; Eltekeh or Elteke, a place in Palestine.

H515.definition: from אֵל and תָּקַן; God (is) straight; Eltekon, a place in Palestine.

H516.definition: from אַל and שָׁחַת; 'Thou must not destroy'; probably the opening words to a popular song.

H517.definition: a primitive word; a mother (as the bond of the family); in a wide sense (both literally and figuratively (like father)).

H518.definition: a primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!; interrogative, whether?; or conditional, if, although; also Oh that!, when; hence, as a negative, not.
H518.kjv: (and, can-, doubtless, if, that) (not), [phrase] but, either, [phrase] except, [phrase] more(-over if, than), neither, nevertheless, nor, oh that, or, [phrase] save (only, -ing), seeing, since, sith, [phrase] surely (no more, none, not), though, [phrase] of a truth, [phrase] unless, [phrase] verily, when, whereas, whether, while, [phrase] yet.

H519.definition: apparently a primitive word; a maidservant or female slave.

H520.definition: prolonged from אֵם; properly, a mother (i.e. unit of measure, or the fore-arm (below the elbow), i.e. a cubit; also a door-base (as a bond of the entrance).
H520.kjv: cubit, [phrase] hundred (by exchange for מֵאָה), measure, post.

H522.definition: the same as אַמָּה; Ammah, a hill in Palestine.

H523.definition: from the same as אֵם; a collection, i.e. community of persons.

H525.definition: from אָמַן, probably in the sense of training; like אָמָן; skilled, i.e. an architect.

H526.definition: the same as אָמוֹן; Amon, the name of three Israelites.

H527.definition: a variation for הָמוֹן; a throng of people.

H528.definition: of Egyptian derivation; used only as an adjunct of נֹא; Amon (i.e. Ammon or Amn), a deity of Egypt.

H529.definition: from אָמַן; established, i.e. (figuratively) trusty; also (abstractly) trustworthiness.

H530.definition: or (shortened) אֱמֻנָה; feminine of אֵמוּן; literally firmness; figuratively security; morally fidelity.
H530.kjv: faith(-ful, -ly, -ness, (man)), set office, stability, steady, truly, truth, verily.

H531.definition: from אָמַץ; strong; Amots, an Israelite.

H532.definition: an abbreviated form for אָמוֹן; Ami, an Israelite.

H533.definition: or (shortened) אַמִּץ; from אָמַץ; strong or (abstractly) strength.

H534.definition: apparently from אָמַר (in the sense of self-exaltation); a summit (of a tree or mountain).

H535.definition: a primitive root; to droop; by implication to be sick, to mourn.

H538.definition: from אֵם; gathering-spot; Amam, a place in Palestine.

H539.definition: a primitive root; (Isaiah 30:21; interchangeable with אָמַן, to go to the right hand); properly, to build up or support; to foster as a parent or nurse; figuratively to render (or be) firm or faithful, to trust or believe, to be permanent or quiet; morally to be true or certain.
H539.kjv: hence, assurance, believe, bring up, establish, [phrase] fail, be faithful (of long continuance, stedfast, sure, surely, trusty, verified), nurse, (-ing father), (put), trust, turn to the right.

H541.definition: denominative from יָמִין; to take the right hand road.
H541.kjv: turn to the right. See אָמַן.

H542.definition: from אָמַן (in the sense of training); an expert.

H543.definition: from אָמַן; sure; abstract, faithfulness; adverb, truly.

H545.definition: feminine of אֹמֶן (in the specific sense of training); tutelage.

H546.definition: feminine form of אֹמֶן (in its usual sense); adverb, surely.

H547.definition: feminine active participle of אֹמֶן (in the original sense of supporting); a column.

H548.definition: feminine of אָמֵן; something fixed, i.e. a covenant. an allowance.

H549.definition: the same as אֲמָנָה; Amanah, a mountain near Damascus.

H550.definition: or אֲמִינוֹן; from אָמַן; faithful; Amnon (or Aminon), a son of David.

H551.kjv: indeed, no doubt, surely, (it is, of a) true(-ly, -th).

H553.definition: a primitive root; to be alert, physically (on foot) or mentally (in courage).
H553.kjv: confirm, be courageous (of good courage, stedfastly minded, strong, stronger), establish, fortify, harden, increase, prevail, strengthen (self), make strong (obstinate, speed).

H554.definition: probably from אָמַץ; of a strong color, i.e. red (others fleet).

H557.definition: from אָמַץ; strong; Amtsi, an Israelite.

H558.definition: or אֲמַצְיָהוּ; from אָמַץ and יָהּ; strength of Jah; Amatsjah, the name of four Israelites.

H559.definition: a primitive root; to say (used with great latitude).
H559.kjv: answer, appoint, avouch, bid, boast self, call, certify, challenge, charge, [phrase] (at the, give) command(-ment), commune, consider, declare, demand, [idiom] desire, determine, [idiom] expressly, [idiom] indeed, [idiom] intend, name, [idiom] plainly, promise, publish, report, require, say, speak (against, of), [idiom] still, [idiom] suppose, talk, tell, term, [idiom] that is, [idiom] think, use (speech), utter, [idiom] verily, [idiom] yet.

H561.kjv: answer, [idiom] appointed unto him, saying, speech, word.

H563.definition: (Aramaic) perhaps from אֲמַר (in the sense of bringing forth); a lamb.

H564.definition: from אָמַר; talkative; Immer, the name of five Israelites.

H566.definition: from אִמֵּר; Imri, the name of two Israelites.

H567.definition: probably a patronymic from an unused name derived from אָמַר in the sense of publicity, i.e. prominence; thus, a mountaineer; an Emorite, one of the Canaanitish tribes.

H568.definition: or prolonged אֲמַרְיָהוּ; from אָמַר and יָהּ; Jah has said (i.e. promised); Amarjah, the name of nine Israelites.

H569.definition: of uncertain (perhaps foreign) derivation; Amraphel, a king of Shinar.

H570.definition: time past, i.e.; yesterday or last night.

H571.definition: contracted from אָמַן; stability; (figuratively) certainty, truth, trustworthiness.
H571.kjv: assured(-ly), establishment, faithful, right, sure, true (-ly, -th), verity.

H572.definition: from מָתַח; properly, something expansive, i.e. a bag.

H573.definition: from אֶמֶת; veracious; Amittai, an Israelite.

H574.definition: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to that of מֹתֶן; well-loined (i.e. burly) or mighty.

H575.definition: or אָנָה; contracted from אַיִן; where?; hence, whither?, when?; also hither and thither.

H576.definition: (Aramaic) or אֲנָה; (Aramaic), corresponding to אֲנִיlemma אֲנַי second vowel, corrected to אֲנִי; I.

H577.definition: lemma אָנּאָ second vowel, corrected to אָנָּא; or אָנָּה; apparent contracted from אַהֲבָה and נָא; oh now!.

H579.definition: a primitive root (perhaps rather identical with אָנָה through the idea of contraction in anguish); to approach; hence, to meet in various senses.
H579.kjv: befall, deliver, happen, seek a quarrel.

H581.definition: (Aramaic) or (feminine) אִנִּיןlemma אֵנִּין first vowel, corrected to אִנִּין; (Aramaic), corresponding to הֵם; they.

H582.definition: from אָנַשׁ; properly, a mortal (and thus differing from the more dignified אָדָם); hence,; a man in general (singly or collectively); another, [idiom] (blood-) thirsty, certain, chap(-man).
H582.kjv: divers, fellow, [idiom] in the flower of their age, husband, (certain, mortal) man, people, person, servant, some ([idiom] of them), [phrase] stranger, those, [phrase] their trade. It is often unexpressed in the English versions, especially when used in apposition with another word. Compare אִישׁ.

H583.definition: the same as אֱנוֹשׁ; Enosh, a son of Seth.

H586.definition: (Aramaic) or אֲנַחְנָה; (Aramaic), corresponding to אֲנַחְנוּ; we.

H588.definition: probably from the same root as נַחַר; a gorge or narrow pass; Anacharath, a place in Palestine.

H589.definition: lemma אֲנַי second vowel, corrected to אֲנִי; contracted from אָנֹכִי; I.
H589.kjv: I, (as for) me, mine, myself, we, [idiom] which, [idiom] who.

H590.definition: probably from אָנָה (in the sense of conveyance); -a ship or (collectively) a fleet.

H593.definition: from אָנָה and עַם; groaning of (the) people; Aniam, an Israelite.

H594.definition: probably from an unused root meaning to be narrow; according to most a plumb-line, and to others a hook.

H595.definition: sometimes, aw-no'-kee; a primitive pronoun; I.

H596.definition: a primitive root; to mourn, i.e. complain.

H598.definition: (Aramaic) corresponding to אָנַס; figuratively, to distress.

H599.definition: a primitive root; to breathe hard, i.e. be enraged.

H600.definition: (Aramaic) corresponding to אַף (only in the plural as a singular); the face.

H601.definition: from אָנַף; an unclean bird, perhaps the parrot (from its irascibility).

H604.definition: the same as אֲנָקָה; some kind of lizard, probably the gecko (from its wail).

H605.definition: a primitive root; to be frail, feeble, or (figuratively) melancholy.

H606.definition: (Aramaic) or אֱנַשׁ; (Aramaic), corresponding to אֱנוֹשׁ; a man.

H609.definition: of uncertain derivation; Asa, the name of a king and of a Levite.

H610.definition: from סוּךְ; anointed, i.e. an oil-flask.

H612.definition: from אָסַר; a bond (especially manacles of a prisoner).

H614.definition: or אָסִף; from אָסַף; gathered, i.e. (abstractly) a gathering in of crops.

H615.definition: from אָסַר; bound, i.e. a captive.

H617.definition: the same as אַסִּיר; prisoner; Assir, the name of two Israelites.

H618.definition: from an unused root meaning to heap together; a storehouse (only in the plural).

H619.definition: of uncertain derivation; Asnah, one of the Nethinim.

H620.definition: of foreign derivation; Osnappar, an Assyrian king.

H621.definition: of Egyptian derivation; Asenath, the wife of Joseph.

H622.definition: a primitive root; to gather for any purpose; hence, to receive, take away, i.e. remove (destroy, leave behind, put up, restore, etc.).
H622.kjv: assemble, bring, consume, destroy, felch, gather (in, together, up again), [idiom] generally, get (him), lose, put all together, receive, recover (another from leprosy), (be) rereward, [idiom] surely, take (away, into, up), [idiom] utterly, withdraw.

H623.definition: from אָסַף; collector; Asaph, the name of three Israelites, and of the family of the first.

H624.definition: passive participle of אָסַף; collected (only in the plural), i.e. a collection of offerings).

H626.definition: from אָסַף; a collection of people (only adverbial).

H627.definition: lemma אֲסֻפָה missing dagesh, corrected to אֲסֻפָּה; feminine of אָסֻף; a collection of (learned) men (only in the plural).

H628.definition: by reduplication from אָסֻף; gathered up together, i.e. a promiscuous assemblage (of people).

H630.definition: of Persian derivation; Aspatha, a son of Haman.

H631.definition: a primitive root; to yoke or hitch; by analogy, to fasten in any sense, to join battle.
H631.kjv: bind, fast, gird, harness, hold, keep, make ready, order, prepare, prison(-er), put in bonds, set in array, tie.

H632.definition: or אִסָּר; from אָסַר; an obligation or vow (of abstinence).

H633.definition: (Aramaic) corresponding to אֱסָר in a legal sense; an interdict.

H634.definition: of foreign derivation; Esar-chaddon, an Assyrian king.

H635.definition: of Persian derivation; Ester, the Jewish heroine.

H636.definition: (Aramaic) corresponding to עֵץ; a tree or wood.

H637.definition: a primitive particle; meaning accession (used as an adverb or conjunction); also or yea; adversatively though.
H637.kjv: also, [phrase] although, and (furthermore, yet), but, even, [phrase] how much less (more, rather than), moreover, with, yea.

H639.definition: from אָנַף; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also (from the rapid breathing in passion) ire.
H639.kjv: anger(-gry), [phrase] before, countenance, face, [phrase] forebearing, forehead, [phrase] (long-) suffering, nose, nostril, snout, [idiom] worthy, wrath.

H640.definition: a primitive root (rather a denominative from אֵפוֹד); to gird on (the ephod).

H641.definition: the same as אֵפוֹד shortened; Ephod, an Israelite.

H642.definition: feminine of אֵפוֹד; a girding on (of the ephod); hence, generally, a plating (of metal).

H643.definition: apparently of foreign derivation; a pavilion or palace-tent.

H644.definition: a primitive root; to cook, especially to bake.

H645.definition: or אֵפוֹא; from פֹּה; strictly a demonstrative particle, here; but used of time, now or then.

H646.definition: rarely אֵפֹד; probably of foreign derivation; a girdle; specifically the ephod or highpriest's shoulder-piece; also generally, an image.

H647.definition: perhaps from פּוּחַ; breeze; Aphiach, an Israelite.

H648.definition: from the same as אָפֵל (in the sense of weakness); unripe.

H649.definition: dual of אַף; two nostrils; Appajim, an Israelite.

H650.definition: from אָסַף; properly, containing, i.e. a tube; also a bed or valley of astream; also a strong thing or a hero.
H650.kjv: brook, channel, mighty, river, [phrase] scale, stream, strong piece.

H651.definition: from an unused root meaning to set as the sun; dusky.

H652.definition: from the same as אָפֵל; dusk.

H653.definition: feminine of אָפֵל; duskiness, figuratively, misfortune; concrete, concealment.

H654.definition: from פָּלַל; judge; Ephlal, an Israelite.

H655.definition: from an unused root meaning to revolve; a turn, i.e. a season.

H656.definition: a primitive root; to disappear, i.e. cease.
H656.kjv: be clean gone (at an end, brought to nought), fail.

H657.definition: from אָפֵס; (sometimes like פָּעַל); cessation, i.e. an end (especially of the earth); often used adverb, no further; also the ankle (in the dual), as being the extremity of the leg or foot.
H657.kjv: ankle, but (only), end, howbeit, less than nothing, nevertheless (where), no, none (beside), not (any, -withstanding), thing of nought, save(-ing), there, uttermost part, want, without (cause).

H658.definition: from אֶפֶס and the plural of דָּם; boundary of blood-drops; Ephes-Dammim, a place in Palestine.

H659.definition: from an unused root probably meaning to breathe; properly, a breath, i.e. nothing.

H660.definition: from אֵפַע (in the sense of hissing); an asp or other venomous serpent.

H662.definition: a primitive root; to contain, i.e. (reflex.) abstain.

H663.definition: or אֲפִיק; from אָפַק (in the sense of strength); fortress; Aphek (or Aphik), the name of three places in Palestine.

H664.definition: feminine of אֲפֵק; fortress; Aphekah, a place in Palestine.

H665.definition: from an unused root meaning to bestrew; ashes.

H666.definition: from the same as אֵפֶר (in the sense of covering); a turban.

H667.definition: from פָּרַח (in the sense of bursting the shell); the brood of a bird.

H668.definition: probably of Egyptian derivation; a palanquin.

H669.definition: dual of masculine form of אֶפְרָת; double fruit; Ephrajim, a son of Joseph; also the tribe descended from him, and its territory.

H670.definition: (Aramaic) of foreign origin (only in the plural); an Apherasite or inhabitant of an unknown region of Assyria.

H671.definition: (Aramaic) or אֲפַרְסַתְכַי; (Aramaic), of foreign origin (only in the plural); an Apharsekite or Apharsathkite, an unknown Assyrian tribe.

H672.definition: or אֶפְרָתָה; from פָּרָה; fruitfulness; Ephrath, another name for Bethlehem; once (Psalm 132:6) perhaps for Ephraim; also of an Israelitish woman.

H673.definition: patrial from אֶפְרָת; an Ephrathite or an Ephraimite.

H674.definition: (Aramaic) of Persian origin; revenue; others at the last.

H675.definition: lemma אֶצְבּוֹ missing final nun, corrected to אֶצְבּוֹן; or אֶצְבֹּן; of uncertain derivation; Etsbon, the name of two Israelites.

H676.definition: from the same as צֶבַע (in the sense of grasping); something to sieze with, i.e. a finger; by analogy, a toe.

H678.definition: from אָצַל (in its secondary sense of separation); an extremity (Isaiah 41:9), also a noble.

H679.definition: from אָצַל (in its primary sense of uniting); a joint of the hand (i.e. knuckle); also (according to some) a party-wall (Ezekiel 41:8).

H680.definition: a primitive root; properly, to join; used only as a denominative from אֵצֶל; to separate; hence, to select, refuse, contract.

H681.definition: from אָצַל (in the sense of joining); a side; (as a preposition) near.
H681.kjv: at, (hard) by, (from) (beside), near (unto), toward, with. See also בֵּית הָאֵצֶל.

H682.definition: from אָצַל; noble; Atsel, the name of an Israelite, and of a place in Palestine.

H683.definition: from אָצַל and יָהּ prolonged; Jah has reserved; Atsaljah, an Israelite.

H684.definition: from an unused root probably meaning to be strong; strength (i.e. strong); Otsem, the name of two Israelites.

H685.definition: a variation from צְעָדָה; properly, a step-chain; by analogy, a bracelet.

H686.definition: a primitive root; to store up.

H687.definition: from אָצַר; treasure; Etser, an Idumaean.

H688.definition: from קִדָּה; burning, i.e. a carbuncle or other fiery gem.

H689.definition: probably from אָנַק; slender, i.e. the ibex.

H690.definition: probably for אֲרִי; lion; Ara, an Israelite.

H692.definition: from אֶרְאֵל; heroic; Areli (or an Arelite, collectively), an Israelite and his descendants.

H694.definition: from אָרַב; ambush; Arab, a place in Palestine.

H697.definition: from רָבָה; a locust (from its rapid increase).

H698.definition: feminine of אֹרֶב (only in the plural); ambuscades.

H699.definition: feminine participle passive of אָרַב (as if for lurking); a lattice; (by implication) a window, dovecot (because of the pigeon-holes), chimney (with its apertures for smoke), sluice (with openings for water).

H700.definition: plural of אֲרֻבָּה; Arubboth, a place in Palestine.

H701.definition: patrial from אֲרָב; an Arbite or native of Arab.

H704.definition: the same as אַרְבַּע; Arba, one of the Anakim.

H707.definition: a primitive root; to plait or weave.

H708.definition: from אָרַג; a weaving; a braid; also a shuttle.

H709.definition: from the same as רֶגֶב; stony; Argob, a district of Palestine.

H712.definition: perhaps from רָגַז (in the sense of being suspended),; a box (as a pannier).

H713.definition: of foreign origin; purple (the color or the dyed stuff).

H714.definition: from an unused root probably meaning to wander; fugitive; Ard, the name of two Israelites.

H715.definition: from the same as אַרְדְּ; roaming; Ardon, an Israelite.

H716.definition: patronymic from אַרְדְּ; an Ardite (collectively) or descendant of Ard.

H719.definition: probably from רוּד; a refuge for the roving; Arvad, an island-city of Palestine.

H720.definition: an orthographical variation of אַרְוַד; fugitive; Arod, an Israelite.

H721.definition: patrial from אַרְוַד; an Arvadite or citizen of Arvad.

H722.definition: patronymic from אַרְוָדִי; an Arodite or descendant of Arod.

H723.definition: or אֲרָיָה; from אָרָה (in the sense of feeding); a herding-place for an animal.

H724.definition: or אֲרֻכָה; feminine passive participle of אָרַךְ (in the sense of restoring to soundness); wholeness (literally or figuratively).

H725.definition: a variation of רוּמָה; height; Arumah, a place in Palestine.

H726.definition: a clerical error for אֱדֹמִי; an Edomite (as in the margin).

H727.definition: or אָרֹן; from אָרָה (in the sense of gathering); a box.

H728.definition: or (by transposition) אוֹרְנָה; or אַרְנִיָּהlemma אַנִיָּה missing resh, corrected to אַרְנִיָּה; all by orthographical variation for אׇרְנָן; Aravnah (or Arnijah or Ornah), a Jebusite.

H729.definition: a primitive root; to be firm; used only in the passive participle as a denominative from אֶרֶז; of cedar.

H730.definition: from אָרַז; a cedar tree (from the tenacity of its roots).

H733.definition: from אָרַח; way faring; Arach, the name of three Israelites.

H734.definition: from אָרַח; a well-trodden road (literally or figuratively); also a caravan.
H734.kjv: manner, path, race, rank, traveller, troop, (by-, high-) way.

H736.definition: feminine active participle of אָרַח; a caravan.

H737.definition: feminine passive participle of אָרַח (in the sense of appointing); a ration of food.

H738.definition: or (prolonged) [ar-yay']; from אָרָה (in the sense of violence); a lion.

H739.definition: or אֲרִאֵל; from אֲרִי and אֵל; lion of God; i.e. heroic.

H740.definition: xlit ʼĂrîʼêl corrected to ʼĂrîyʼêl; the same as אֲרִיאֵל; Ariel, a symbolical name for Jerusalem, also the name of an Israelite.

H741.definition: either by transposition for אֲרִיאֵל or, more probably, an orthographical variation for הַרְאֵל; the altar of the temple.

H742.definition: of Persian origin; Aridai, a son of Haman.

H743.definition: of Persian origin; Aridatha, a son of Haman.

H745.definition: the same as אֲרִי; lion; Arjeh, an Israelite.

H746.definition: of foreign origin; Arjok, the name of two Babylonians.

H747.definition: of Persian origin; Arisai, a son of Haman.

H748.definition: a primitive root; to be (causative, make) long (literally or figuratively).
H748.kjv: defer, draw out, lengthen, (be, become, make, pro-) long, [phrase] (out-, over-) live, tarry (long).

H749.definition: (Aramaic) properly, corresponding to אָרַךְ, but used only in the sense of reaching to a given point; to suit.

H751.definition: from אָרַךְ; length; Erek, a place in Babylon.

H755.definition: (Aramaic) from an unused root corresponding to רָכַב (in the sense of bending the knee); the knee.

H756.definition: (Aramaic) patrial from אֶרֶךְ; an Arkevite (collectively) or native of Erek.

H757.definition: patrial from another place (in Palestine) of similar name with אֶרֶךְ; an Arkite or native of Erek.

H758.definition: from the same as אַרְמוֹן; the highland; Aram or Syria, and its inhabitants; also the name of the son of Shem, a grandson of Nahor, and of an Israelite.

H759.definition: from an unused root (meaning to be elevated); a citadel (from its height).

H760.definition: xlit ʼĂram Tsôbâh corrected to ʼĂram Tsôwbâh; from אֲרָם and צוֹבָא; Aram of Tsoba (or Coele-Syria).

H761.definition: patrial from אֲרָם; an Aramite or Aramaean.

H762.definition: feminine of אֲרַמִּי; (only adverbial) in Aramean.
H762.kjv: in the Syrian language (tongue), in Syriac.

H763.definition: from אֲרָם and the dual of נָהָר; Aram of (the) two rivers (Euphrates and Tigris) or Mesopotamia.

H764.definition: from אַרְמוֹן; palatial; Armoni, an Israelite.

H765.definition: from רָנַן; stridulous; Aran, an Edomite.

H766.definition: from the same as אֲרָן (in the sense of strength); the ash tree (from its toughness).

H767.definition: the same as אֹרֶן; Oren, an Israelite.

H769.definition: or אַרְנֹןlemma אַרְנן missing vowel, corrected to אַרְנֹן; from רָנַן; a brawling stream; the Arnon, a river east of the Jordan, also its territory.

H770.definition: probably from the same as אַרְנוֹן; noisy; Arnan, an Israelite.

H771.definition: probably from אֹרֶן; strong; Ornan, a Jebusite.

H772.definition: (Aramaic) corresponding to אֶרֶץ; the earth; by implication (figuratively) low.

H774.definition: from רָפַד; spread out; Arpad, a place in Syria.

H775.definition: probably of foreign origin; Arpakshad, a son of Noah; also the region settled by him.

H776.definition: from an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth (at large, or partitively a land).
H776.kjv: [idiom] common, country, earth, field, ground, land, [idiom] natins, way, [phrase] wilderness, world.

H777.definition: from אֶרֶץ; earthiness; Artsa, an Israelite.

H778.definition: (Aramaic) by transmutation for אֲרַע; the earth.

H780.definition: of foreign origin; Ararat (or rather Armenia).

H781.definition: a primitive root; to engage for matrimony.

H782.definition: from אָרַשׂ (in the sense of desiring to possess); a longing for.

H783.definition: or אַרְתַּחְשַׁשְׁתְּא; or by permutation אַרְתַּחְשַׁסְתְּא; of foreign origin; Artachshasta (or Artaxerxes), a title (rather than name) of several Persian kings.

H784.definition: a primitive word; fire (literally or figuratively).

H786.definition: identical (in origin and formation) with אֵשׁ; entity, used only adverbially, there is or are.
H786.kjv: are there, none can. Compare יֵשׁ.

H787.definition: (Aramaic) corresponding (by transposition and abbreviation) to אֲשׁוּיָה; a foundation.

H788.definition: probably from the same as שֹׁבֶל; flowing; Ashbel, an Israelite.

H789.definition: patronymic from אַשְׁבֵּל; an Ashbelite (collectively) or descendant of Ashbel.

H790.definition: lemma אֶשְּׁבָּן extra dagesh, corrected to אֶשְׁבָּן; probably from the same as שֶׁבְנָא; vigorous; Eshban, an Idumaean.

H791.definition: from שָׁבַע; adjurer; Asbea, an Israelite.

H792.definition: from אִישׁ and בַּעַל; man of Baal; Eshbaal (or Ishbosheth), a son of Saul.

H793.definition: from an unused root meaning to pour; an outpouring.

H795.definition: from שָׁדַד; ravager; Ashdod, a place in Palestine.

H796.definition: patrial from אַשְׁדּוֹד; an Ashdodite (often collectively) or inhabitant of Asdod.

H797.definition: feminine of אַשְׁדּוֹדִי; (only adverb) in the language of Ashdod.

H798.definition: from the plural of אֲשֵׁדָה and פִּסְגָּה with the article interposed; ravines of the Pisgah; Ashdoth-Pisgah, a place east of the Jordan.

H799.definition: from אֵשׁ and דָּת; a fire-law.

H801.definition: the same as אֶשָּׁה, but used in a liturgical sense; properly, a burnt-offering; but occasionally of any sacrifice.

H802.definition: feminine of אִישׁ or אֱנוֹשׁ; irregular plural, נָשִׁים;(used in the same wide sense as אֱנוֹשׁ); a woman.
H802.kjv: (adulter) ess, each, every, female, [idiom] many, [phrase] none, one, [phrase] together, wife, woman. Often unexpressed in English.

H803.definition: feminine passive participle from an unused root meaning to found; foundation.

H804.definition: or אַשֻּׁר; apparently from אָשַׁר (in the sense of successful); Ashshur, the second son of Shem; also his descendants and the country occupied by them (i.e. Assyria), its region and its empire.
H804.kjv: Asshur, Assur, Assyria, Assyrians. See אָשֻׁר.

H805.definition: or אַשּׁוּרִי; from a patrial word of the same form as אַשּׁוּר; an Ashurite (collectively) or inhabitant of Ashur, a district in Palestine.

H806.definition: probably from שָׁחַר; black; Ashchur, an Israelite.

H807.definition: of foreign origin; Ashima, a deity of Hamath.

H808.definition: from the same as אֵשׁ (in the sense of pressing down firmly; compare אֲשׁוּיָה); a (ruined) foundation.

H809.definition: feminine of אָשִׁישׁ; something closely pressed together, i.e. a cake of raisins or other comfits.

H810.definition: from an unused root (probably meaning to bunch together); a testicle (as a lump).

H811.definition: or אֶשְׁכֹּל; probably prolonged from אֶשֶׁךְ; a bunch of grapes or other fruit.

H812.definition: the same as אֶשְׁכּוֹל; Eshcol, the name of an Amorite, also of a valley in Palestine.

H813.definition: of foreign origin; Ashkenaz, a Japhethite, also his descendants.

H815.definition: from a root of uncertain signification; a tamarisk tree; by extension, a grove of any kind.

H816.definition: or אָשֵׁם; a primitive root; to be guilty; by implication to be punished or perish.
H816.kjv: [idiom] certainly, be(-come, made) desolate, destroy, [idiom] greatly, be(-come, found, hold) guilty, offend (acknowledge offence), trespass.

H817.definition: from אָשַׁם; guilt; by implication, a fault; also a sin-offering.

H818.definition: from אָשַׁם; guilty; hence, presenting asin-offering.

H819.definition: feminine of אָשָׁם; guiltiness, a fault, the presentation of asin-offering.

H821.definition: or אַשׁמוּרָה; or אַשׁמֹרֶת; (feminine) from שָׁמַר; a night watch.

H822.definition: apparently from an unused root (probably meaning to leave interstices); a latticed window.

H823.definition: probably a variation for יְשָׁנָה; Ashnah, the name of two places in Palestine.

H824.definition: from שָׁעַן; support; Eshan, a place in Palestine.

H825.definition: from an unused root (probably meaning to lisp, i.e. practice enchantment); a conjurer.

H827.definition: perhaps (feminine) from the same as אַשָּׁף (in the sense of covering); a quiver or arrow-case.

H828.definition: of foreign origin; Ashpenaz, a Babylonian eunuch.

H829.definition: of uncertain derivation; a measured portion.

H830.definition: or אַשְׁפּוֹת; or שְׁפֹת; (contraction) plural of a noun of the same form as אַשְׁפָּה, from שָׁפָה (in the sense of scraping); a heap of rubbish or filth.

H831.definition: probably from שָׁקַל in the sense of weighing-place (i.e. mart); Ashkelon, a place in Palestine.

H832.definition: patrial from אַשְׁקְלוֹן; Ashkelonite (collectively) or inhabitant of Ashkelon.

H833.definition: or אָשֵׁר; a primitive root; to be straight (used in the widest sense, especially to be level, right, happy); figuratively, to go forward, be honest, proper.
H833.kjv: (call, be) bless(-ed, happy), go, guide, lead, relieve.

H834.definition: a primitive relative pronoun (of every gender and number); who, which, what, that; also (as an adverb and a conjunction) when, where, how, because, in order that, etc..
H834.kjv: [idiom] after, [idiom] alike, as (soon as), because, [idiom] every, for, [phrase] forasmuch, [phrase] from whence, [phrase] how(-soever), [idiom] if, (so) that ((thing) which, wherein), [idiom] though, [phrase] until, [phrase] whatsoever, when, where ([phrase] -as, -in, -of, -on, -soever, -with), which, whilst, [phrase] whither(-soever), who(-m, -soever, -se). As it is indeclinable, it is often accompanied by the personal pronoun expletively, used to show the connection.

H835.definition: from אָשַׁר; happiness; only in masculine plural construction as interjection, how happy!.

H836.definition: from אָשַׁר,; happy; Asher, a son of Jacob, and the tribe descended from him, with its territory; also a place in Palestine.

H838.definition: or אַשֻּׁר; from אָשַׁר in the sense of going; a step.

H839.definition: contracted for תְּאַשּׁוּר; the cedar tree or some other light elastic wood.

H840.definition: by orthographical variation from אָשַׁר and אֵל; right of God; Asarel, an Israelite.

H841.definition: from the same as אֲשַׂרְאֵל; right toward God; Asarelah, an Israelite.
